{"query":"Cantina Apex AppSec agent xz utils vulnerability disclosure report 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1952,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.zerodayinitiative.com/advisories/published/","title":"Published Advisories - TrendAI™ Zero Day Initiative™ (ZDI)","content":"The following is a list of all publicly disclosed vulnerabilities discovered by TrendAI™ Zero Day Initiative™ (ZDI) researchers."},{"url":"https://mondoo.com/vulnerability-intelligence/state-of-vulnerabilities-2026","title":"2026 State of Vulnerabilities - Annual Security Report | Mondoo Vulnerability Intelligence","content":"2026 State of Vulnerabilities
This report synthesizes data from 768,000+ security advisories, cross-referenced with major industry reports including Verizon DBIR, Mandiant M-Trends, and CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.
The findings challenge conventional assumptions about vulnerability prioritization and highlight the growing gap between disclosure velocity and organizational response capabilities.
Protecting cloud infrastructure requires CIS Benchmarks and CSPM tools, not vulnerability scanners.
Vulnerability exploitation now accounts for 20% of all breaches (Verizon DBIR, up 34% YoY) and 21.3% of intrusions (ENISA).
Generic injection vulnerabilities grew from 302 in 2024 to 2,556 in 2025.
Windows published 1,244 CVEs with 29 KEVs (2.33% exploitation rate) - and 76% were zero-days exploited before disclosure. macOS had 730 CVEs with 8 KEVs (1.1% exploitation).
The EU Vulnerability Database (EUVD) launched under NIS2 Directive.
PentestGPT (USENIX Security 2024) provided academic validation that LLMs can effectively assist penetration testing.
The data tells a clear story: attackers are faster than ever (5-day average time-to-exploit), targeting specific infrastructure (edge devices account for 44% of zero-days), and exploiting gaps in visibility (193K malware packages bypass CVE tracking entirely).

These commits are made under author Lasse Collin."},{"url":"https://www.akamai.com/blog/security-research/critical-linux-backdoor-xz-utils-discovered-what-to-know","title":"XZ Utils Backdoor — Everything You Need to Know, and What You Can Do | Akamai","content":"CVE-2024-3094 is a vulnerability discovered in the open-source library XZ Utils that stems from malicious code that was pushed into the library by one of its maintainers.
XZ Utils, and its underlying library liblzma, are open-source projects that implement the lzma compression and decompression.
For a more detailed explanation of the backdoor parts, you can read Andres Freund's post on openwall.
There was only one thing that stopped that from happening — Andres Freund.
Discover CVE-2026-48282, a critical path traversal vulnerability in Adobe ColdFusion."},{"url":"https://www.thezdi.com/blog/2026/7/14/the-july-2026-security-update-review","title":"Zero Day Initiative — The July 2026 Security Update Review","content":"The patch for Cold Fusion even clocks in with a CVSS 9.9 bug.
It's a missing-authentication flaw, meaning an unauthenticated attacker can hit it over the network with no user interaction required.
CVE-2026-56169 — Windows Admin Center Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability — Important — 8.1 — No — No — EoP
CVE-2026-55145 — Outlook Copilot Tampering Vulnerability — Moderate — 6.3 — No — No — Tampering
CVE-2026-48561 (Microsoft Copilot RCE, 9.6) and CVE-2026-50380 (Windows GDI+ RCE, 9.6) round out the near-top tier.

tavily.raw: {"query":"Cantina Apex AppSec agent xz utils vulnerability disclosure report 2026","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.sentinelone.com/blog/xz-utils-backdoor-threat-actor-planned-to-inject-further-vulnerabilities","title":"XZ Utils Backdoor | Threat Actor Planned to Inject Further Vulnerabilities","content":"Threat Hunting World Championship 2026 - Qualifying Stage. Further, the backdoor scripts included system checks to guarantee that the object files were solely injected into Debian and Fedora distributions. SentinelOne analyzed the technical implementation of the xz backdoor and the differences between the two versions. In the first iteration of the compromise (version 5.6.0), the actor successfully added code to the xz repository that enabled injection of the backdoor on Debian and Fedora distributions. However, the second iteration (version 5.6.1) adds significantly more maturity by introducing the ability to execute additional shell scripts during the build phase via binary test blobs, presumably to make future updates to the backdoor less suspicious. The injection of malicious shell scripts occurs during the execution of the `configure` command, which then inserts code inside the Makefile to build and replace object files with backdoor-infected counterparts. This file orchestrates minor modifications and conceals the extraction and execution of the Stage 1 backdoor file, `bad-3-corrupt_lzma2.xz`.","score":0.614464,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/cantinasecurity_your-dependencies-carry-15-years-of-bugs-activity-7445214696405909504-AuXx","title":"XZ Utils 15-Year Bug Caught by AI AppSec Tool Apex","content":"Cantina disclosed a bug in XZ Utils that has existed since before 2010. A relative directory path traversal vulnerability (CVE-2026-34926) in Trend Micro’s Apex One platform has been exploited in zero-day attacks, the company confirmed. ### About Trend Micro Apex One. Trend Micro Apex One is a security platform that protects all the devices in an organization from cyber threats. CVE-2026-34926 and seven additional vulnerabilities affecting Apex One security agents were disclosed by Trend Micro last week, but only CVE-2026-34926 was flagged as actively exploited. “This vulnerability is only exploitable on the on-premise version of Apex One and a potential attacker must have access to the Apex One Server and already obtained administrative credentials to the server via some other method to exploit this vulnerability,” the company noted. Trend Micro has yet to share details about the attack its experts responded to, but has urged customers to update their on-prem Apex One server deployments and security agents as soon as possible.","score":0.46104163,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.akamai.com/blog/security-research/critical-linux-backdoor-xz-utils-discovered-what-to-know","title":"XZ Utils Backdoor — Everything You Need to Know, and ...","content":"Akamai acquires LayerX, delivering end-to-end security and real-time AI usage control to any browser.
